commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

subcommand was redundant — remove it entirely.

-268 lines, +26 lines. Build from scars, not speculation.

commit.message:
feat: add QR code generation history tracking system

Implement comprehensive QR code generation history tracking for admin accountability and analytics:

Backend (Code.gs):
- Add QR_GENERATION_HISTORY sheet to track all QR code creation
- Implement logQRGeneration() API to record QR generation details
- Implement getQRGenerationHistory() API to retrieve generation history
- Update redeemPointsQR() to increment redemption count when QR is scanned
- Add proper error handling with try-catch blocks

Frontend (checkin.html):
- Modify generatePointsQR() to async and call backend logging API
- Update loadAdminPointsHistory() to fetch and display QR history
- Add visual progress bars showing redemption status (green/yellow/red)
- Display admin name, timestamp, points, note, and usage progress
- Auto-load admin history when switching to dashboard tab
- Fix double booking issue by removing duplicate addPoints() call

Features:
- Tracks when QR codes were created and by which admin
- Shows how many times each QR has been redeemed vs. total uses
- Color-coded progress bars (0-49% green, 50-99% yellow, 100% red)
- Last 50 QR codes displayed, sorted by newest first
- Prevents data loss with proper error handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use suited hero hand for hand type evaluation

evaluate_hand was called with normalized hand name (e.g. "ATo") instead
of the raw suited hand (e.g. "AcTh"), making flush and flush draw
detection impossible on suited boards. Now uses hero_hand_raw when
4-char suits are available.

Example: AcTh on Jc7cQcJs9c was labeled "中對 (J)" instead of
"同花 (A-Q-J-9-7)" — causing LLM to think hero was a bluff-catcher
when actually holding the nut flush.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Mobile-first redesign + keyword UX improvements

Mobile layout:
- DashboardShell.tsx: new client wrapper wiring TopBar + Sidebar + MobileBottomNav
- TopBar: logo left, hamburger right on mobile; all other content hidden (md:hidden)
- MobileBottomNav: fixed bottom bar with 5 tool icons (Keywords, Trending, Niche, Titles, Description)
- Sidebar: mobile drawer now includes Pricing/Contact/About/My Account links
- globals.css: force light mode always; input/textarea/select text forced to #111827 (fixes dark mode gray text on iPhone)

Mobile-only suppressions:
- FilterPanel hidden on mobile (keywords page)
- FeedbackWidget button hidden on mobile
- Trending 'Full Research Tool' button hidden on mobile
- Niche Finder suggestion chips hidden on mobile
- Competition Score + Results columns hidden in matching keywords tables (both search results and breakdown page)
- Keywords page: stacked search bar (input above, button below), emoji subtitle lines, shorter description
- Keyword breakdown: Save Keyword button stacked below title; View on TpT button stacked below text
- Saved Keywords empty state: shorter text on mobile

Keyword UX improvements:
- scrapeKeywordLongTail: smart product-type detection (13 types) with contextual suffixes per type
- FilterPanel: replaced with TpT-style pill dropdowns, auto-search on selection, 8 filter categories
- Search placeholder: '3rd grade math review spiral with answers' on both mobile and desktop
- Search button renamed to 'Search Keywords'
- API auth removed from keyword-breakdown and dashboard/opportunities routes (public read-only)
- Keyword cache cleared to fix stale long-tail results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add release system with changelog, Slack notification, and CODEOWNERS (#39)

## Summary

- **Release pipeline**: `/release` Claude command generates AI-written
CHANGELOG entries, interactive Python script (`uv run
scripts/release.py`) handles version bumping, committing, tagging, and
pushing
- **Slack notifications**: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on tag push
(`v*`) with manual `workflow_dispatch` for re-triggering; updated
`send-changelog-to-slack.py` with tag-range support and version-aware
headers
- **CODEOWNERS**: All 8 contributors required as reviewers for every PR

## New files (7)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `VERSION` | Source of truth for marketplace version (`0.4.0`) |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Template with marker for `/release` entries |
| `.claude/commands/release.md` | `/release` slash command for
AI-generated changelogs |
| `Taskfile.dist.yaml` | `task validate`, `task release`, `task
release:dry` |
| `scripts/release.py` | Interactive release TUI (colored output,
prompts, dry-run) |
| `.github/workflows/release-notify.yml` | Slack notification on tag
push + manual dispatch |
| `.github/CODEOWNERS` | PR review requirements |

## Modified files (1)

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `scripts/send-changelog-to-slack.py` | Tag-range priority for
`get_commit_range()`, version-aware Slack header/footer |

## Release flow

```
/release minor              → Claude writes CHANGELOG.md entry
uv run scripts/release.py   → Validates → bumps VERSION + marketplace.json → commits → tags → pushes
Tag push (v*)               → GitHub Actions → Slack notification
```

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `/release` in Claude Code — verify CHANGELOG entry generation
- [ ] Run `uv run scripts/release.py --dry-run` — verify preflight
checks and output
- [ ] Run `task validate` — verify plugin validation
- [ ] Push a test tag and verify GitHub Actions workflow triggers
- [ ] Verify `workflow_dispatch` works for manual Slack re-trigger

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update skills.md §18 HPC: fix cayuga, prioritize rapids, add conda-hang fixes

- rapids-singlecell: promote to default on GPU nodes (was optional); always
  use over scanpy pp.neighbors/umap/leiden when GPU is available
- §18.6 rewritten as "Package Installation on HPC: Handling Slow/Hanging
  Conda" with 5-option decision tree:
  1. pip / uv (default for Python packages)
  2. rapids via NVIDIA PyPI index (pip --extra-index-url pypi.nvidia.com)
  3. mamba/micromamba with Lustre lockfile fix (use_locks: false)
  4. Spack (system-level compiled libs only)
  5. Apptainer container (nuclear option)
- Add conda hang to common pitfalls table

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: run TALL-masks before orthogonalization to prevent blurry output

Orthogonalized diffs have uncorrelated element-wise distributions, which
caused TALL-masks to classify every position as "selfish" (agreement=1
everywhere). This zeroed all consensus diffs, making the merge operate on
zeros and producing only an un-normalized selfish patchwork — resulting in
extremely blurry output on enhanced/maximum quality.

Fix: swap the order so TALL-masks runs first on the original correlated
diffs (correct selfish detection), then orthogonalize only the consensus
portion. Applied to both non-TIES and TIES quality pipelines.

commit.message:
Fix WeasyPrint lib loading on Linux/Railway with nix store pre-loading

ctypes.util.find_library() on Linux uses ldconfig, not LD_LIBRARY_PATH,
so setting it at runtime wasn't enough. Now pre-load .so files from
/nix/store/*/lib via ctypes.cdll.LoadLibrary (same approach as the macOS
fix). Also set LD_LIBRARY_PATH in the nixpacks start command so the
dynamic linker has the paths before Python even starts.
